description_long: This is a compact, rectangular orange first-aid kit with a hard plastic shell, showing visible scuffs and wear. The kit opens on a hinge to reveal a neatly organized interior with six cylindrical containers, each capped and color-coded, likely for different medical supplies or medications. The inside of the lid features a printed instruction sheet in Russian, detailing the contents and their uses. The exterior of the lid is embossed with Cyrillic text and a circular emblem, indicating its official or standardized nature. The kit is designed for portability and quick access, suitable for emergency use in urban environments. Its robust construction suggests it is intended to protect its contents from impact and environmental exposure.
description: A rugged orange plastic first-aid kit with embossed Cyrillic labeling, containing color-coded medical vials and an interior instruction sheet.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open kit with six color-coded vials and a detailed instruction sheet inside the lid.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the plain, scuffed back of the kit and the partially open lid.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the thickness of the kit and the embossed text on the lid.
description_view_3: Angled front view displaying the organized vials, open lid, and clear emergency labeling on the exterior.
